_meta.note: FDA regulatory guidance: endpoints, biomarkers, and designation criteria by indication. Keys use snake_case (lowercased, spaces→underscores).

criteria: Preliminary clinical evidence of substantial improvement over existing therapy on a clinically significant endpoint
breakthrough_therapy.timing: After Phase I; FDA targets 60-day review of application
breakthrough_therapy.benefits: More intensive FDA guidance, rolling review, organisational commitment from senior FDA staff
breakthrough_therapy.notes: Does NOT guarantee approval; ~30% of BTD drugs fail in Phase III

criteria: Potential to address unmet medical need
fast_track.benefits: More frequent FDA meetings, rolling review eligibility, potential priority review
fast_track.notes: Broadest designation; easier to obtain than BTD

criteria: Significant improvement in safety or efficacy over standard of care
priority_review.notes: Granted to ~50% of new oncology drugs

criteria: Surrogate or intermediate endpoint reasonably likely to predict clinical benefit
accelerated_approval.benefits: Earlier approval based on surrogate; confirmatory trial required post-approval
accelerated_approval.notes: FDORA 2022: FDA can now require confirmatory trial start before AA granted

orphan_drug.criteria_us: Affects fewer than 200,000 persons in the US
orphan_drug.criteria_eu: Affects fewer than 5 in 10,000 persons in EU (≈ 165,000)
benefits: 50% tax credit on clinical trial costs
orphan_drug.notes: Can be granted alongside BTD/Fast Track

rare_pediatric_disease.criteria: Serious or life-threatening disease affecting <200,000 persons under 18
rare_pediatric_disease.benefits: Priority Review Voucher (PRV) worth $100M+ on secondary market upon approval

non_small_cell_lung_cancer.required_biomarker: EGFR, ALK, ROS1, KRAS G12C, MET exon 14, RET, NTRK, BRAF V600E, PD-L1 TPS

common_endpoints_by_setting.1L_metastatic: OS (preferred) or PFS with OS trend

non_small_cell_lung_cancer.fda_notes: Biomarker-stratified enrichment required for targeted therapies; PD-L1 ≥50% threshold for first-line IO monotherapy
non_small_cell_lung_cancer.fda_preference: FDA prefers OS as primary; PFS acceptable only if OS is immature with strong HR trend. Biomarker-unselected designs now disfavored — enrichment by actionable alteration required for targeted therapies.

breast_cancer.primary_endpoint: Invasive Disease-Free Survival (iDFS) [adjuvant]; PFS or OS [metastatic]
breast_cancer.required_biomarker: HER2 status (IHC/FISH), HR status (ER/PR IHC), PIK3CA mutation, ESR1 mutation (ESR1 for endocrine-resistant)

common_endpoints_by_setting.neoadjuvant: pathological Complete Response (pCR); iDFS as co-primary

breast_cancer.fda_notes: HER2 low (IHC 1+ or 2+/ISH-) now a recognized subgroup per ADC data (T-DXd). ESR1 liquid biopsy required for ESR1 degrader programs.
breast_cancer.fda_preference: FDA prefers iDFS for adjuvant; pCR as surrogate for accelerated approval (neoadjuvant). HER2-low (IHC 1+ or 2+/ISH-) must be pre-specified subgroup per 2023 draft guidance.

hormone_receptor_positive_breast_cancer.required_biomarker: ER/PR IHC, ESR1 mutation (circulating tumor DNA for second-line)
hormone_receptor_positive_breast_cancer.fda_notes: ESR1 liquid biopsy CDx required for ESR1-targeted therapies post-aromatase inhibitor
hormone_receptor_positive_breast_cancer.fda_preference: FDA prefers iDFS ≥3-year follow-up for HR+/HER2- adjuvant. For endocrine-resistant metastatic, PFS acceptable; ESR1 liquid biopsy pre-specification required for ESR1-targeting agents.

colorectal_cancer.required_biomarker: RAS/BRAF status (KRAS/NRAS exons 2-4, BRAF V600E), MSI/MMR status, HER2
colorectal_cancer.fda_notes: Wild-type RAS required for EGFR antibody use (cetuximab, panitumumab). MSI-H is IO biomarker.
typical_designations: BTD for MSI-H-selected or BRAF V600E
colorectal_cancer.fda_preference: FDA prefers OS for front-line metastatic; PFS acceptable with strong OS trend. RAS/BRAF biomarker stratification required. MSI-H/dMMR is a required stratification factor since 2017.

hepatocellular_carcinoma.fda_notes: Child-Pugh A preferred for trials; mRECIST used for ORR (not RECIST 1.1). First-line: sorafenib/lenvatinib comparison required.
hepatocellular_carcinoma.fda_preference: FDA prefers OS; PFS/TTP acceptable if OS immature. BCLC stage C enrichment and Child-Pugh A liver function required. Etiology (HBV/HCV/NASH) should be stratified.

prostate_adenocarcinoma.required_biomarker: FOXA1 or PTEN loss status; HRR/BRCA status for PARP inhibitors; AR splice variant (ARV7) for enzalutamide/abiraterone resistance
prostate_adenocarcinoma.fda_notes: Integrated CDx (Companion Diagnostic) required for Phase III initiation for PARP inhibitors. AR targeting: ARSI comparative arm required.
prostate_adenocarcinoma.fda_preference: FDA prefers rPFS for metastatic CRPC (nmCRPC: MFS). OS increasingly required as confirmatory. PSA alone insufficient; imaging-based RECIST + bone scan required.

diffuse_large_b_cell_lymphoma.primary_endpoint: Event-Free Survival (EFS) or Progression-Free Survival (PFS); OS for later lines
diffuse_large_b_cell_lymphoma.required_biomarker: CD20, IPI score, cell-of-origin (GCB/ABC), MYC/BCL2 double-expressor status
diffuse_large_b_cell_lymphoma.fda_notes: PET-CT assessed CR rate via Lugano criteria accepted for accelerated approval in relapsed/refractory. LBCL subtype now stratified (DLBCL NOS vs. HGBL).
diffuse_large_b_cell_lymphoma.fda_preference: FDA prefers EFS from randomisation for front-line; CR rate by PET-CT (Lugano 2014) for R/R accelerated approval. CMR at end of treatment as key secondary.

follicular_lymphoma.primary_endpoint: PFS or CR rate (early lines); ORR/DoR for later lines
follicular_lymphoma.required_biomarker: CD20, CD79b (for polatuzumab), FL grade (1-3a vs 3b)
follicular_lymphoma.fda_notes: Surrogate endpoint (ORR/CR) acceptable for accelerated approval in ≥2nd line R/R. PFS required for earlier lines.
follicular_lymphoma.fda_preference: FDA prefers PFS; CR rate by Lugano criteria acceptable for accelerated approval. MRD negativity emerging as co-primary for fixed-duration regimens.

multiple_myeloma.primary_endpoint: PFS (early/mid lines); OS (later lines with demonstrated PFS benefit)
multiple_myeloma.required_biomarker: FISH cytogenetics (del17p, t(4;14)), BCMA expression, CD38
multiple_myeloma.fda_notes: MRD negativity emerging as surrogate endpoint. BCMA CDx not yet required but expected for BCMA-targeted therapies.
multiple_myeloma.fda_preference: FDA prefers PFS with MRD negativity as co-primary for front-line. Stringent CR (sCR) by IMWG criteria required. Minimal residual disease (NGS, 10⁻⁵ sensitivity) now expected.

acute_myeloid_leukemia.primary_endpoint: OS; CR/CRi rate for accelerated approval in R/R
acute_myeloid_leukemia.required_biomarker: FLT3 (ITD/TKD), IDH1, IDH2, TP53, NPM1, cytogenetics
acute_myeloid_leukemia.fda_notes: FLT3 and IDH CDx required for targeted therapies. OS preferred; CR rate acceptable for accelerated approval.
acute_myeloid_leukemia.fda_preference: FDA prefers OS; CR/CRi acceptable for accelerated approval in R/R. ELN 2022 risk stratification required as stratification factor. MRD negativity (NGF or PCR) as key secondary.

alzheimer_disease.primary_endpoint: CDR-SB (Clinical Dementia Rating Sum of Boxes) — co-primary with iADRS or ADAS-Cog
alzheimer_disease.required_biomarker: Amyloid PET (or CSF Aβ42/tau) for amyloid-targeting agents; APOE4 genotype for safety monitoring (ARIA risk)
alzheimer_disease.fda_notes: Amyloid-positive confirmed biomarker required. ARIA (amyloid-related imaging abnormalities) monitoring protocol required. FDA accepts amyloid PET as reasonably likely surrogate for accelerated approval. CDR-SB minimum 18-month follow-up.
alzheimer_disease.fda_preference: FDA requires amyloid-confirmed population (PET or CSF). CDR-SB minimum 18-month follow-up. ARIA safety monitoring protocol mandatory. Accelerated approval via amyloid clearance requires confirmatory OS/CDR-SB trial.

parkinson_disease.primary_endpoint: MDS-UPDRS Part II+III (motor and functional); DAT-SPECT for neuroprotection claims
parkinson_disease.required_biomarker: LRRK2 mutation (kinase inhibitors), GBA1 variant, SNCA (alpha-synuclein); DaT scan for diagnosis
parkinson_disease.fda_notes: LRRK2 and GBA1 companion diagnostics likely required for precision PD programs. Disease modification claims require 'futility-proof' withdrawal design or delayed-start.
parkinson_disease.fda_preference: FDA requires disease-modification claims use delayed-start or withdrawal design. MDS-UPDRS Part III minimum 12-week efficacy window. LRRK2/GBA1 CDx required for precision programs.

multiple_sclerosis.primary_endpoint: Annualized Relapse Rate (ARR) [RRMS]; Confirmed Disability Progression (CDP) at 12/24 weeks [PPMS/SPMS]
multiple_sclerosis.required_biomarker: CD20 (for B-cell depleting agents), NfL (serum neurofilament light as emerging biomarker)
multiple_sclerosis.fda_notes: Anti-CD20 standard of care. Phase III must compare against IFN-β or glatiramer acetate (or active comparator). NfL not yet accepted as primary endpoint.
multiple_sclerosis.fda_preference: FDA prefers ARR for RRMS; CDP at 12 and 24 weeks for PPMS/SPMS. No evidence of disease activity (NEDA-3/4) as supportive endpoint. BTD pathway preferred for progressive forms.

amyotrophic_lateral_sclerosis.primary_endpoint: ALSFRS-R slope (joint-rank analysis per FDA 2023 guidance); Survival
amyotrophic_lateral_sclerosis.required_biomarker: SOD1 mutation (for tofersen), FUS, TDP-43, C9orf72 expansion; neurofilament NfL
amyotrophic_lateral_sclerosis.fda_notes: FDA 2023 guidance allows joint-rank statistical approach. SOD1-targeted therapy requires genetic CDx. NfL accepted as surrogate for accelerated approval (tofersen precedent).
amyotrophic_lateral_sclerosis.fda_preference: FDA prefers ALSFRS-R joint-rank analysis (2023 guidance) combining function + survival. Survival is co-primary for disease-modifying claims. Biomarker enrichment (SOD1, FUS, TDP-43) pre-specification required.

atrial_fibrillation.primary_endpoint: Stroke/systemic embolism rate; time in therapeutic range (TTR) for anticoagulants
atrial_fibrillation.required_biomarker: CHA2DS2-VASc score, HAS-BLED score; no molecular CDx required
atrial_fibrillation.fda_notes: Non-inferiority design vs. warfarin or DOAC standard. MACE composite must include stroke. Bleeding events adjudicated by ISTH criteria.
atrial_fibrillation.fda_preference: FDA prefers non-inferiority vs. warfarin (TTR ≥65%) for novel anticoagulants. Time to first stroke/SE as primary. ISTH major bleeding as key safety endpoint.

heart_failure.primary_endpoint: Composite MACE: CV death + HF hospitalization (KCCQ ≥5 pts optional co-primary); or CV death alone
heart_failure.required_biomarker: EF categorization: HFrEF (EF <40%), HFmrEF (40-50%), HFpEF (>50%); NT-proBNP for eligibility
heart_failure.fda_notes: HFpEF increasingly important; FDA accepts win-ratio analysis for hierarchical composite. LVEF subgroup stratification mandatory.
heart_failure.fda_preference: FDA prefers composite MACE (CV death + HF hospitalization). KCCQ total symptom score acceptable as co-primary per 2023 draft. HFpEF trials must stratify by EF (≥50% vs 41-49%).

chronic_kidney_disease.primary_endpoint: Sustained ≥40% eGFR decline + ESKD + CV death + renal death (kidney composite)
chronic_kidney_disease.required_biomarker: eGFR baseline, UACR, CKD etiology (DKD vs. IgA vs. FSGS)
chronic_kidney_disease.fda_notes: FDA 2023 guidance: eGFR slope acceptable as reasonably likely surrogate for accelerated approval in rare CKD subtypes. UACR reduction not standalone endpoint.
chronic_kidney_disease.fda_preference: FDA prefers kidney composite (sustained ≥40% eGFR decline + ESKD + renal/CV death). Rate of eGFR slope acceptable as surrogate. UACR as key secondary; SGLT2 active comparator increasingly expected.

rheumatoid_arthritis.required_biomarker: RF, anti-CCP (ACPA); seronegative vs. seropositive stratification required
rheumatoid_arthritis.fda_notes: TNF-IR population required in most Phase III programs (MTX-IR or bDMARD-IR). Radiographic non-progression must be demonstrated. Head-to-head vs. TNF-α inhibitor preferred.
rheumatoid_arthritis.fda_preference: FDA prefers ACR20/50/70 at Week 24; DAS28-CRP remission (<2.6) as co-primary. Radiographic progression (modified Sharp/van der Heijde) required for structural claims. JAK inhibitor programs require CV/VTE safety data.

systemic_lupus_erythematosus.primary_endpoint: SRI-4 (SLE Responder Index) or BICLA at Week 52
systemic_lupus_erythematosus.required_biomarker: Anti-dsDNA, complement C3/C4, SLEDAI score; IFN gene signature for type-I IFN targeting agents
systemic_lupus_erythematosus.fda_notes: Active disease (SLEDAI ≥6 or ≥4) and on stable background SoC required for enrollment. IFN-high patient enrichment may be required for IFN pathway therapies.
systemic_lupus_erythematosus.fda_preference: FDA prefers SRI-4 or BICLA at Week 52. Low disease activity state (LLDAS) and steroid-sparing as key secondaries. Lupus nephritis trials use renal response (uPCR <0.5 + eGFR ≥60).

inflammatory_bowel_disease.primary_endpoint: Clinical remission (Mayo score or modified Mayo for UC; CDAI<150 for CD) at Week 8 (induction) and Week 52 (maintenance)
inflammatory_bowel_disease.required_biomarker: Fecal calprotectin, CRP; anti-integrin: α4β7 for gut-selective agents; JAK inhibitors: CBC monitoring
inflammatory_bowel_disease.fda_notes: FDA requires dual-component remission (stool frequency + rectal bleeding for UC; CDAI for CD). Endoscopic remission increasingly required as co-primary. Biologics-experienced separate from naive.
inflammatory_bowel_disease.fda_preference: FDA prefers combined clinical + endoscopic remission. Patient-reported outcomes (stool frequency, rectal bleeding) must be separately powered. Histologic remission as key secondary per 2022 draft guidance.

ulcerative_colitis.primary_endpoint: Modified Mayo Score clinical remission (stool frequency + rectal bleeding + endoscopic subscore) at Week 8 and 52
ulcerative_colitis.required_biomarker: None specific; calprotectin and CRP for disease monitoring
ulcerative_colitis.fda_notes: Endoscopic subscore ≤1 required for remission definition. Biologic-naive and biologic-experienced arms must be separated.
ulcerative_colitis.fda_preference: FDA requires endoscopic remission (Mayo endoscopic subscore 0 or 1 with friability absent). PRO remission (stool frequency + rectal bleeding) must be co-primary. Central reading of endoscopy required.

crohn_disease.primary_endpoint: CDAI <150 (clinical remission) at Week 12 (induction) + Week 52 (maintenance); SES-CD for endoscopic
crohn_disease.required_biomarker: CRP, fecal calprotectin; MRI for transmural healing endpoint
crohn_disease.fda_notes: FDA increasingly requires endoscopic and histologic data alongside clinical. Transmural remission emerging as aspirational endpoint.
crohn_disease.fda_preference: FDA prefers PRO-based clinical remission (CDAI may be replaced by stool frequency + AP score per 2023 guidance). Endoscopic response (SES-CD ≥50% reduction) as key secondary. Fistula trials require separate trial.

atopic_dermatitis.primary_endpoint: IGA 0/1 (Investigator Global Assessment) AND EASI-75 (≥75% reduction from baseline) at Week 16
atopic_dermatitis.required_biomarker: None; type-2 biomarkers (IgE, eosinophils, TARC) used for enrichment but not required by FDA
atopic_dermatitis.fda_notes: Both IGA 0/1 AND EASI-75 required as co-primary. Pruritus NRS as key secondary (≥4-point improvement). Long-term safety data on lymphoma risk for JAK inhibitors required.
atopic_dermatitis.fda_preference: FDA requires both IGA 0/1 AND EASI-75 as dual primary endpoints. PRO (Peak Pruritus NRS ≥4 reduction) must be powered. Long-term safety (malignancy, CV, JAK programs) required at 5 years.

asthma.primary_endpoint: Annualized Exacerbation Rate (AER); FEV1 improvement as key secondary
asthma.required_biomarker: Blood eosinophil count (≥300/μL threshold for IL-5/IL-4/13 agents); FeNO; periostin
asthma.fda_notes: Severe asthma (≥2 exacerbations/year on high-dose ICS) required. Biomarker-enriched populations improve trial efficiency. FDA accepts AER as primary with FEV1 key secondary.
asthma.fda_preference: FDA prefers AER with ≥12-month follow-up; FEV1 as key secondary. Biomarker enrichment (IgE, blood eosinophils, FeNO, IL-4/13) required for precision biologics. Type 2 low asthma programs need separate study.

idiopathic_pulmonary_fibrosis.primary_endpoint: Rate of FVC decline (mL/year) at 52 weeks
idiopathic_pulmonary_fibrosis.required_biomarker: UIP pattern on HRCT or surgical biopsy (required for diagnosis); MUC5B genotype (rs35705950) for risk stratification
idiopathic_pulmonary_fibrosis.fda_notes: FVC decline rate is gold standard primary. All-cause mortality requires very large N. Progressor enrichment (FVC 40-90%) recommended. Nintedanib/pirfenidone background therapy now mandatory in maintenance arms.
idiopathic_pulmonary_fibrosis.fda_preference: FDA prefers rate of FVC decline (mL/year) at 52 weeks. Decline ≥10% FVC or death as key binary secondary. 6MWT and SGRQ as supportive PROs. Active comparator vs. nintedanib/pirfenidone now expected in Phase 3.

hemophilia_a.primary_endpoint: Annualized Bleed Rate (ABR); zero-bleed rate as key secondary
hemophilia_a.required_biomarker: FVIII activity level, inhibitor status; hemophilia severity (severe: FVIII <1%)
hemophilia_a.fda_notes: Intra-patient comparison (non-interventional lead-in) acceptable as control for rare disease. Non-inferiority against prophylaxis comparator standard.
hemophilia_a.fda_preference: FDA prefers ABR with zero-bleed rate as key secondary. Head-to-head comparison vs. standard prophylaxis (emicizumab or FVIII) increasingly expected. Inhibitor development monitoring required.

paroxysmal_nocturnal_hemoglobinuria.primary_endpoint: Transfusion avoidance; hemoglobin stabilization; LDH normalization
paroxysmal_nocturnal_hemoglobinuria.required_biomarker: PNH clone size (GPI-deficient RBCs ≥5%); LDH as pharmacodynamic marker
paroxysmal_nocturnal_hemoglobinuria.fda_notes: Complement inhibitor standard of care; new agents must show non-inferiority to eculizumab. Transfusion independence as key secondary.
paroxysmal_nocturnal_hemoglobinuria.fda_preference: FDA prefers transfusion avoidance + LDH normalisation. Breakthrough hemolysis events as key safety secondary. eculizumab/ravulizumab non-inferiority design standard for complement inhibitors.

wet_age_related_macular_degeneration.primary_endpoint: BCVA change from baseline (ETDRS letters) at 12 months; proportion avoiding ≥15-letter loss
wet_age_related_macular_degeneration.required_biomarker: OCT-confirmed CNV; VEGF-A, Ang-2 (for dual pathway agents)
wet_age_related_macular_degeneration.fda_notes: Non-inferiority to ranibizumab/aflibercept standard. Extended dosing intervals (Q12W, Q16W) as differentiation strategy. Binocular trials not acceptable as primary.
wet_age_related_macular_degeneration.fda_preference: FDA prefers BCVA ≥15-letter gain at 12 months; proportion avoiding ≥15-letter loss as co-primary. Central subfield thickness (OCT) as key secondary. Extended dosing interval (treat-and-extend) pre-specification preferred.

geographic_atrophy.primary_endpoint: Rate of GA lesion growth (mm²/year by fundus autofluorescence) at 12 months
geographic_atrophy.required_biomarker: FAF-confirmed GA; complement factor H genotype (CFH Y402H) for complement pathway therapies
geographic_atrophy.fda_notes: GA area growth rate accepted as reasonably likely surrogate (accelerated approval precedent: pegcetacoplan). Vision endpoints difficult due to natural variability.
typical_designations: Accelerated Approval via GA lesion growth rate
geographic_atrophy.fda_preference: FDA accepts GA lesion growth rate (mm²/year by FAF) as reasonably likely surrogate for accelerated approval. Visual function (LLVA, reading speed) as confirmatory endpoint for full approval.

type_2_diabetes.primary_endpoint: HbA1c reduction from baseline at 24-26 weeks (superiority or non-inferiority); CV outcomes (MACE) for labels
type_2_diabetes.required_biomarker: HbA1c ≥7.5% at baseline; eGFR for renal dosing; ASCVD risk for CV claims
type_2_diabetes.fda_notes: All new T2D agents must complete CV outcomes trial (CVOT) to rule out CV harm (MACE). Renal outcomes now co-primary in DKD programs. Weight management claims require separate study.
type_2_diabetes.fda_preference: FDA prefers HbA1c at 24-26 weeks; CV outcome trial (CVOT) required for full approval (MACE non-inferiority vs. placebo). eGFR trajectory as key secondary for SGLT2/GLP-1 programs.

obesity.primary_endpoint: Percentage body weight loss from baseline at 68-72 weeks + proportion achieving ≥5% weight loss
obesity.required_biomarker: BMI ≥30 (or ≥27 with weight-related comorbidity); no molecular biomarker required
obesity.fda_notes: FDA 2023 CV Outcomes guidance: ≥5% mean weight loss + superiority on ≥5% responder rate required. SURMOUNT-MMRM or similar designs accepted. CV outcomes label requires dedicated MACE trial.
obesity.fda_preference: FDA requires ≥5% mean weight loss vs. placebo AND proportion achieving ≥5% loss (responder analysis) as co-primary. CV outcomes increasingly required (SELECT precedent). Weight regain post-discontinuation must be characterised.

spinal_muscular_atrophy.primary_endpoint: HFMSE (Hammersmith Functional Motor Scale Expanded) or CHOP-INTEND (infantile onset); ventilation-free survival
spinal_muscular_atrophy.required_biomarker: SMN1 gene copy number (0 copies for SMA type), SMN2 copy number (modifier of severity)
spinal_muscular_atrophy.fda_notes: SMN2 copy count genetic test required as CDx. Pre-symptomatic treatment standard emerging. Nusinersen/onasemnogene/risdiplam standard of care.
spinal_muscular_atrophy.fda_preference: FDA prefers motor milestone achievement for infantile SMA (CHOP-INTEND); HFMSE for later-onset. Event-free survival (death/permanent ventilation) as key secondary. Newborn screening stratification required.

duchenne_muscular_dystrophy.primary_endpoint: 6-Minute Walk Test (6MWT) distance; North Star Ambulatory Assessment (NSAA)
duchenne_muscular_dystrophy.required_biomarker: Dystrophin exon mutation (CDx for exon-skipping); dystrophin protein expression (Western blot/IHC for gene therapy)
duchenne_muscular_dystrophy.fda_notes: Exon 51/53/45 skipping agents: exon mutation CDx mandatory. Accelerated approval via dystrophin restoration (surrogate). Confirmatory functional trial required.
duchenne_muscular_dystrophy.fda_preference: FDA accepts functional outcomes (6MWT, NSAA) for accelerated approval; decline in function as co-primary for confirmatory trials. Exon-skipping programs require dystrophin quantification (western blot, IHC) as surrogate.

cystic_fibrosis.primary_endpoint: ppFEV1 (percent predicted FEV1) change from baseline at 24 weeks
cystic_fibrosis.required_biomarker: CFTR mutation genotype (F508del homozygous vs. heterozygous vs. minimal function); CDx required for all CFTR modulators
cystic_fibrosis.fda_notes: CFTR mutation panels are the archetype CDx program. Sweat chloride as pharmacodynamic biomarker. FEV1 + sweat chloride + exacerbation composite common.
cystic_fibrosis.fda_preference: FDA prefers ppFEV1 at 24 weeks; sweat chloride as pharmacodynamic marker. CFTR modulator programs require stratification by mutation class. Pulmonary exacerbation rate as key secondary.

hiv_infection.primary_endpoint: Proportion with HIV-1 RNA <50 copies/mL at Week 48
hiv_infection.required_biomarker: HIV-1 genotype resistance testing (tropism assay for CCR5 antagonists); HBV/HCV co-infection status
hiv_infection.fda_notes: Non-inferiority to optimized background regimen standard. Switch studies: stable suppression required at entry. Long-acting injectable: 4-week and 8-week dosing separate registrations.
hiv_infection.fda_preference: FDA prefers HIV RNA <50 copies/mL at Week 48 (snapshot algorithm). Resistance development and tolerability as key secondaries. Long-acting injectable programs require non-inferiority vs. daily oral SOC (dolutegravir-based).

hepatitis_b.primary_endpoint: HBsAg loss at 48/96 weeks; ALT normalization; HBV DNA suppression (<29 IU/mL)
hepatitis_b.required_biomarker: HBV genotype, HBsAg quantification, HBeAg status; viral load
hepatitis_b.fda_notes: Functional cure (HBsAg loss) is the aspirational endpoint. Regulatory science evolving — FDA may accept HBsAg decline + maintained viral suppression as surrogate for accelerated approval.
hepatitis_b.fda_preference: FDA prefers functional cure (HBsAg loss ± seroconversion) as primary for finite-duration programs; HBV DNA suppression for chronic suppression. Finite treatment designs preferred over indefinite therapy.

hepatitis_c.primary_endpoint: Sustained Virologic Response at Week 12 post-treatment (SVR12)
hepatitis_c.required_biomarker: HCV genotype (1-6) for genotype-specific agents; NS5A/NS5B resistance testing
hepatitis_c.fda_notes: SVR12 = cure. All-oral DAA standard achieved SVR12 >95%. New agents must show non-inferiority. Pangenotypic label preferred.
hepatitis_c.fda_preference: FDA requires SVR12 as primary endpoint (regulatory standard since 2012). Direct-acting antiviral programs require genotype stratification and prior treatment history stratification.

melanoma.required_biomarker: BRAF V600E/K status (mandatory CDx for BRAF/MEK inhibitors); PD-L1 (optional); tumor mutational burden (TMB)
melanoma.fda_notes: BRAF V600E CDx required. IO (PD-1/CTLA-4 combo): survival endpoint. BRAF-targeted: PFS required; OS benefit strengthens label.
melanoma.fda_preference: FDA prefers PFS or OS for unresectable/metastatic; RFS for adjuvant. BRAF V600 mutation status required as stratification factor. Combination IO programs require contribution-of-components data.

ovarian_cancer.primary_endpoint: PFS (front-line maintenance); ORR/DoR (relapsed); OS for full approval
ovarian_cancer.required_biomarker: BRCA1/2 mutation (germline and somatic); HRD score (Myriad myChoice CDx); platinum sensitivity
ovarian_cancer.fda_notes: BRCA CDx required for PARP inhibitor front-line maintenance. HRD testing separates population. Platinum-sensitive vs. resistant must be separate studies.
ovarian_cancer.fda_preference: FDA prefers PFS for front-line maintenance (BRCA/HRD stratification required); OS as confirmatory. ORR for accelerated approval in platinum-resistant. Germline + somatic BRCA and HRD CDx pre-specification required.

pancreatic_ductal_adenocarcinoma.primary_endpoint: OS (gold standard); PFS acceptable with OS trend for later lines
pancreatic_ductal_adenocarcinoma.required_biomarker: KRAS G12C/D/V, BRCA1/2, NTRK, POLE; DPC4/SMAD4 (prognostic)
pancreatic_ductal_adenocarcinoma.fda_notes: Notoriously difficult indication; FDA open to innovative designs (randomized Phase II with OS as primary). KRAS G12C/D are targetable mutations. Gemcitabine+nab-paclitaxel or FOLFIRINOX comparators.
pancreatic_ductal_adenocarcinoma.fda_preference: FDA prefers OS for all lines; PFS acceptable only with strong OS trend for later lines. Biomarker stratification (KRAS G12C, BRCA1/2, NTRK) increasingly required. Functional status (ECOG 0-1 enrichment) pre-specified.

urothelial_carcinoma.primary_endpoint: OS or PFS (1L cisplatin-ineligible or 2L+); ORR for accelerated approval
urothelial_carcinoma.required_biomarker: PD-L1 (CPS ≥5 or ≥10 for 1L IO in cisplatin-ineligible); FGFR2/3 alterations (CDx for FGFR inhibitors); NECTIN4; Trop2
urothelial_carcinoma.fda_notes: FGFR CDx required for erdafitinib. EV+pembro combination changed 1L standard. ADC (EV, sacituzumab) driving label expansion without biomarker.
urothelial_carcinoma.fda_preference: FDA prefers OS or PFS for 1L cisplatin-ineligible; ORR for accelerated approval in 2L+. PD-L1 and FGFR3 status as pre-specified stratification factors. EV-pembrolizumab active comparator precedent.

gastric_gastroesophageal_junction_cancer.required_biomarker: HER2 IHC/ISH (required for trastuzumab); PD-L1 CPS (≥1, ≥5 thresholds); MSI-H status; FGFR2b (IHC for bemarituzumab)
gastric_gastroesophageal_junction_cancer.fda_notes: HER2 testing mandatory for all mG/GEJ. CPS threshold varies by IO agent. FGFR2b CDx emerging.
gastric_gastroesophageal_junction_cancer.fda_preference: FDA prefers OS for metastatic (HER2+ and HER2-). DFS for perioperative/adjuvant. HER2 IHC/ISH, PD-L1 CPS ≥5, and MSI-H as required stratification factors.

renal_cell_carcinoma.required_biomarker: IMDC risk score; PD-L1 (optional); VHL mutation (belzutifan); carbonic anhydrase IX
renal_cell_carcinoma.fda_notes: IO + TKI combinations now 1L standard. VHL mutation CDx required for belzutifan. Adjuvant: DFS with OS trend required for pembrolizumab-type approval.
renal_cell_carcinoma.fda_preference: FDA prefers OS or PFS for metastatic (IMDC risk stratification required). DFS for adjuvant (sunitinib/pembrolizumab precedent). PD-L1 and VHL mutation status as stratification factors.

endometrial_cancer.required_biomarker: MMR/MSI status (IHC/PCR); POLE mutation (ultramutated subtype); HER2 (for ADC); p53 (TCGA classifier)
endometrial_cancer.fda_notes: MMR-d/MSI-H dramatically responsive to IO (dostarlimab, pembrolizumab). pMMR remains challenging — carboplatin/paclitaxel backbone. FIGO 2023 staging update expected to influence endpoints.
endometrial_cancer.fda_preference: FDA prefers OS for front-line metastatic; PFS acceptable with OS trend. MMR/MSI status as mandatory stratification factor. HER2 IHC required for HER2+ subgroup pre-specification.

chronic_lymphocytic_leukemia.primary_endpoint: PFS (treatment-naive and R/R); OS emerging as standard for high-risk
chronic_lymphocytic_leukemia.required_biomarker: IGHV mutation status, del17p, TP53 mutation, del11q, del13q; CD20 for anti-CD20 agents
chronic_lymphocytic_leukemia.fda_notes: BTK inhibitors (ibrutinib/acalabrutinib/zanubrutinib) standard. MRD negativity emerging as surrogate in fixed-duration regimens. Del17p/TP53 defines ultra-high risk.
chronic_lymphocytic_leukemia.fda_preference: FDA prefers PFS with OS trend; MRD-undetectable rate as key secondary. TP53/del(17p) and IGHV mutation status required as stratification factors. BTKi resistance mutations (C481S) must be characterised.

mantle_cell_lymphoma.primary_endpoint: ORR (CR+PR) for accelerated approval R/R; PFS or OS for front-line
mantle_cell_lymphoma.required_biomarker: CCND1 translocation, SOX11, Ki-67; CD20; TP53 mutation (high-risk)
mantle_cell_lymphoma.fda_notes: High unmet need supports accelerated approval via ORR/CR. BTK inhibitors and venetoclax+obinutuzumab combinations standard.
mantle_cell_lymphoma.fda_preference: FDA prefers ORR (CR+PR) by Lugano for accelerated approval in R/R; PFS or OS for front-line. Ki-67 proliferation index and TP53 mutation as stratification factors. MRD by ctDNA as co-primary emerging.

glioblastoma.primary_endpoint: OS (gold standard); PFS acceptable if OS immature
glioblastoma.required_biomarker: IDH1/2 mutation (WHO 2021 grade separator); MGMT promoter methylation (predicts TMZ benefit); EGFR amplification; TERT promoter
glioblastoma.fda_notes: MGMT methylation stratification mandatory. IDH-mutant (grade 3-4) now separate entity from IDH-wildtype GBM. Bevacizumab use complicates RECIST — iRANO criteria used.
glioblastoma.fda_preference: FDA prefers OS as gold standard; PFS acceptable as primary with OS as key secondary. MGMT promoter methylation, IDH mutation, and EGFR amplification as required stratification factors. Pseudoprogression must be addressed per protocol.

thyroid_cancer.primary_endpoint: PFS or ORR (differentiated thyroid cancer); OS (anaplastic)
thyroid_cancer.required_biomarker: RET mutation/fusion (CDx for selpercatinib/pralsetinib); BRAF V600E (CDx); NTRK fusion
thyroid_cancer.fda_notes: RET CDx required for targeted therapies. ATC (anaplastic): BTD pathway common given median OS <6 months. Differentiated: RECIST 1.1 with iodine-131 refractoriness criteria.
thyroid_cancer.fda_preference: FDA prefers PFS or ORR for differentiated thyroid cancer; OS for anaplastic. BRAF V600E, RET fusion, NTRK fusion as CDx-required stratification factors for targeted therapies.

huntington_disease.primary_endpoint: Composite Unified Huntington's Disease Rating Scale (cUHDRS) — integrating TFC, SDMT, TMS, and verbal fluency; or Total Functional Capacity (TFC) alone for more advanced disease
huntington_disease.required_biomarker: CAG repeat length (diagnostic/eligibility criterion); mHTT protein in CSF; plasma NfL (pharmacodynamic/surrogate biomarker for ASO therapies)
huntington_disease.fda_notes: Landmark FDA 2015 guidance: cUHDRS as primary. For gene silencing (ASO/siRNA): CSF mHTT reduction as reasonably likely surrogate may support accelerated approval. ENROLL-HD natural history normative data required for trial context. Intraparenchymal or intrathecal delivery trials require additional safety monitoring plan.
huntington_disease.fda_preference: FDA prefers cUHDRS (composite: TFC + UHDRS-TMS + SDMT + verbal fluency) for disease modification. CAG repeat length as required stratification factor. Biofluid neurofilament light (NfL) as pharmacodynamic biomarker.

neuromyelitis_optica_spectrum_disorder.primary_endpoint: Annualized Relapse Rate (ARR) — time-to-first-relapse as sensitivity analysis
neuromyelitis_optica_spectrum_disorder.required_biomarker: AQP4-IgG seropositivity (required for targeted anti-complement/anti-IL-6 therapies); MOG-IgG for MOGAD distinction
neuromyelitis_optica_spectrum_disorder.fda_notes: AQP4-IgG+ subgroup is the primary regulatory population; MOG-IgG+ (MOGAD) is separate IND. ARR primary endpoint accepted by FDA. Very small indication — Orphan Drug Designation standard. Anti-complement (eculizumab/ravelizumab) and anti-IL-6R (satralizumab/tocilizumab) approved with AQP4+ enrichment.
neuromyelitis_optica_spectrum_disorder.fda_preference: FDA prefers ARR (time to first attack) as primary; Expanded Disability Status Scale (EDSS) as key secondary. AQP4-IgG seropositivity as required enrichment criterion. Open-label extension required for rare disease.

nash.primary_endpoint: NASH resolution without worsening of fibrosis (histological) OR fibrosis improvement ≥1 stage without worsening of NASH (combined histological co-primary at 52 weeks for accelerated approval); all-cause or liver-related death + decompensation for confirmatory
nash.required_biomarker: Liver biopsy (centrally read, NASH CRN scoring) required for histologic endpoints; MRI-PDFF and FIB-4 as non-invasive biomarkers; NASH (NAS ≥4 + fibrosis F1-F3) required for enrollment
nash.fda_notes: FDA 2023 guidance: histological endpoints at 52 weeks for accelerated approval; long-term outcome trial required post-approval. Non-invasive biomarkers (LSM, FIB-4, PDFF) acceptable as supportive data but not yet as primary. Resmetirom approved 2024 with histological endpoint precedent.
nash.fda_preference: FDA prefers histological dual primary (NASH resolution + fibrosis non-worsening OR fibrosis improvement + NASH non-worsening) at 72 weeks. Non-invasive biomarkers (FIB-4, MRE) as key secondaries; FDA 2023 draft accepts MRE-based fibrosis as surrogate for accelerated approval.

mash.primary_endpoint: MASH resolution (NAS ≥2 reduction with no worsening of fibrosis) OR fibrosis improvement ≥1 stage (co-primary) at 52 weeks
mash.required_biomarker: Biopsy-confirmed MASH (NAS ≥4, fibrosis F1b-F3); MRI-PDFF ≥8% for enrollment enrichment
mash.fda_notes: MASH is the updated nomenclature for NASH (metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis). Identical regulatory framework to NASH. FDA accepts MASH/NASH interchangeably in submissions.
mash.fda_preference: FDA prefers MASH resolution without worsening of fibrosis AND/OR fibrosis improvement ≥1 stage without MASH worsening at 72 weeks (dual primary). Liver-related clinical outcomes as confirmatory endpoint for full approval.

primary_progressive_multiple_sclerosis.primary_endpoint: Confirmed Disability Worsening (CDW) at 12 or 24 weeks (CDP) — time to 20% worsening on T25FW or 9HPT as composite
primary_progressive_multiple_sclerosis.required_biomarker: Anti-CD20 level (PK/PD for dosing); NfL as pharmacodynamic biomarker; no CDx required
primary_progressive_multiple_sclerosis.fda_notes: Ocrelizumab (ocrevus) approval in PPMS set precedent using CDP-12 as primary. Very large trials required (600-1000 patients). Very slow progression makes OS/CDP endpoints logistically challenging. PPMS enrichment: baseline T2 lesion burden and Gd-enhancing lesions.
primary_progressive_multiple_sclerosis.fda_preference: FDA prefers CDP at 12 and 24 weeks (confirmed disability worsening). Composite of T25FW + 9HPT + SDMT as supportive. OCB positivity and gadolinium-enhancing lesion burden as stratification factors.

psoriasis.primary_endpoint: PASI 90 (≥90% reduction from baseline PASI) at Week 16; IGA 0/1 as co-primary
psoriasis.required_biomarker: None required; IL-17A, IL-23, TNF pathway defines biologic class
psoriasis.fda_notes: PASI 90 now gold standard (replaced PASI 75 for new labels). Head-to-head vs. IL-17 or IL-23 inhibitor increasingly required for competitive positioning. Long-term safety: TB screening, MACE risk with IL-17A agents (Crohn's contraindication).
psoriasis.fda_preference: FDA requires both PASI 90 AND IGA 0/1 as co-primary endpoints at Week 16. PASI 100 (complete clearance) as key secondary. Long-term maintenance (Week 52) and drug-survival required for IL-17/IL-23 agents.

basal_cell_carcinoma.primary_endpoint: ORR (CR+PR) by investigator assessment at 6 months; DOR as key secondary
basal_cell_carcinoma.required_biomarker: Hedgehog pathway activation (PTCH1 loss, SMO mutation) for targeted therapy; no CDx required (class-wide activity)
basal_cell_carcinoma.fda_notes: Vismodegib and sonidegib approval in locally advanced/metastatic BCC set standard. ORR primary with DOR key secondary. Teratogenicity risk requires pregnancy prevention program (REMS).
basal_cell_carcinoma.fda_preference: FDA prefers ORR by investigator (confirmed CR+PR) at 6 months for hedgehog inhibitors; DOR as key secondary. Histological CR required for complete response designation. Vismodegib/sonidegib active comparator benchmark.
